controls.title: Cryptographic Inventory
controls.description: Maintain a complete inventory of quantum-vulnerable assets (CISA PQC roadmap).
controls.remediation: No action required.

controls.title: Prioritized Migration Plan
controls.description: High-value, long-lived secrets must be prioritized for PQC migration.
controls.remediation: Execute prioritized migration for critical/high assets within the agency PQC timeline.

controls.title: Deprecate Legacy Symmetric/Hash
controls.description: Phase out DES/3DES/AES-128 and MD5/SHA-1.
controls.remediation: Standardize on AES-256-GCM and SHA-256/SHA-3.

reports.summary: FedRAMP: 33% control coverage — 2 failing, 0 partial. Automated remediation guidance attached; this report replaces an estimated 5 hours of manual audit work.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality & Integrity
controls.description: Data in transit must use FIPS-validated, quantum-ready cryptography.
controls.remediation: Deploy hybrid TLS (classical + ML-KEM) and re-issue certs with PQC signatures.

controls.title: Use of Cryptography
controls.description: Only FIPS-approved algorithms with a defined PQC transition may be used.
controls.remediation: Track each algorithm against the NIST PQC transition schedule.

controls.title: System Component Inventory

reports.summary: SOC2: 0% control coverage — 3 failing, 0 partial. Automated remediation guidance attached; this report replaces an estimated 5 hours of manual audit work.

controls.title: Logical Access — Encryption of Data
controls.description: Logical access controls protect information assets with cryptography that must remain resistant to quantum attack.
controls.remediation: Replace quantum-vulnerable public-key algorithms guarding protected data with NIST PQC standards (ML-KEM, ML-DSA).

controls.title: Transmission & Movement of Information
controls.description: Information in transit must be protected with strong, quantum-ready encryption channels.
controls.remediation: Deploy hybrid TLS (classical + ML-KEM) for transmitted data and re-issue certificates with PQC-ready signatures.

controls.title: Detection of Vulnerable Configurations
controls.description: Monitoring must detect deprecated cryptographic primitives that weaken the control environment.
controls.remediation: Retire MD5/SHA-1, DES/3DES/AES-128, and DSA; standardize on SHA-256/SHA-3, AES-256-GCM, and ML-DSA.

reports.summary: PCI-DSS: 0% control coverage — 4 failing, 0 partial. Automated remediation guidance attached; this report replaces an estimated 6 hours of manual audit work.

controls.title: Protect Stored Account Data with Strong Cryptography
controls.description: Stored cardholder data must be rendered unreadable using cryptography that withstands quantum-capable adversaries.
controls.remediation: Migrate stored-data protection to AES-256-GCM and NIST PQC public-key schemes (ML-KEM/ML-DSA); retire legacy ciphers.

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Management Procedures
controls.description: Keys protecting account data must be generated and managed with quantum-resistant key establishment.
controls.remediation: Adopt ML-KEM (Kyber) for key establishment and document a PQC key-rotation plan for all cardholder-data keys.

controls.title: Key Lifecycle & Retirement of Weak Keys
controls.description: Cryptographic keys nearing the end of their secure life, including those broken by quantum attack, must be retired.
controls.remediation: Retire DSA and legacy-hash keys and prioritize critical/high-risk keys for PQC re-keying within the migration window.

controls.title: Strong Cryptography for Transmission
controls.description: PAN transmitted over open networks must use strong, quantum-ready cryptography and trusted certificates.
controls.remediation: Enforce hybrid TLS (classical + ML-KEM) on all transmission paths and re-issue certificates with PQC-ready signatures.

reports.summary: CNSA-2.0: 20% control coverage — 4 failing, 0 partial. Automated remediation guidance attached; this report replaces an estimated 8 hours of manual audit work.

controls.title: Key Establishment — ML-KEM-1024
controls.description: All asymmetric key establishment must use ML-KEM-1024 (FIPS 203); RSA transport and (EC)DH are disallowed.
controls.remediation: Replace RSA key transport and (EC)DH key exchange with ML-KEM-1024.

controls.title: Digital Signatures — ML-DSA-87
controls.description: General-purpose signatures must use ML-DSA-87 (FIPS 204); RSA, ECDSA, EdDSA, and DSA do not qualify.
controls.remediation: Migrate signing to ML-DSA-87; retire RSA/ECDSA/EdDSA/DSA signature keys.

controls.title: Symmetric Encryption — AES-256
controls.description: Symmetric encryption must use AES with 256-bit keys; AES-128/192 and legacy ciphers are insufficient.
controls.remediation: Standardize on AES-256-GCM; remove DES/3DES and sub-256-bit AES.

controls.title: Hashing — SHA-384 / SHA-512
controls.description: Hashing must use SHA-384 or SHA-512; MD5, SHA-1, and SHA-256 do not meet CNSA 2.0.
controls.remediation: No action required.

controls.title: Transition-Timeline Conformance
controls.description: Quantum-vulnerable public-key crypto must be eliminated ahead of the CNSA 2.0 enforcement gates (NSS exclusive by ~2033).
controls.remediation: Schedule removal of every RSA/ECC/DSA/DH usage against the CNSA 2.0 timeline.

assessmentHtml:
QuantumVault

Quantum Readiness
Assessment

Cryptographic asset inventory, post-quantum risk ranking, and compliance gap analysis.
Prepared for: Demo Organization
Scope: Popular OSS libraries — jsonwebtoken · pyjwt · golang-jwt · paramiko · 296 files scanned
Scan date: 2026-06-24
Report generated: 2026-06-24
CONFIDENTIAL — Prepared for Demo Organization. Contains sensitive cryptographic posture information. Do not distribute.

Executive Summary

F
Quantum-posture grade

Demo Organization's estate is materially exposed to "harvest-now, decrypt-later." We inventoried 658 quantum-vulnerable cryptographic assets across 296 files. The majority (96%) rely on Shor-vulnerable public-key cryptography (RSA, ECC, DSA, Diffie-Hellman) — algorithms a cryptographically-relevant quantum computer breaks outright. 168 sit on production data-protection paths that warrant priority migration. Overall quantum-posture grade: F (Critical exposure).

658
Vulnerable assets
168
High priority
296
Files scanned
~4486
Est. migration days

Posture

658 of 658 assets are quantum-vulnerable, 168 high-priority. 0% remediated · 18% avg compliance.

Cryptographic Inventory

Findings by algorithm family. Shor-broken asymmetric crypto is the priority; symmetric/hash are Grover-affected and lower-urgency.

FamilyAssetsShareQuantum impactNIST replacement
ECC (elliptic curve)37056% Broken (Shor)ML-KEM / ML-DSA
RSA22935% Broken (Shor)ML-KEM / ML-DSA
Symmetric (legacy)294% Weakened (Grover)AES-256
Diffie-Hellman213% Broken (Shor)ML-KEM
Asymmetric (PKCS#8, unspecified)61% Broken (Shor)Identify & replace
DSA30% Broken (Shor)ML-DSA

Priority distribution

PriorityAssetsMeaning
critical 0Production secrets on sensitive paths — migrate immediately
high 168Production crypto — prioritize
medium 127Plan migration
low 363Test/vendored or low-exposure — de-prioritized

233 of 658 assets are on production paths; the remainder are test/example or vendored code, de-prioritized for migration.

Selected high-priority findings

AlgorithmLocationPriority
ECDSA/ECDHjsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:11 high
ECDSA/ECDHjsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:12 high
ECDSA/ECDHjsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:13 high
ECDSA (JWT ES)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:5 high
ECDSA (JWT ES)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:11 high
ECDSA (JWT ES)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:12 high
ECDSA (JWT ES)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:13 high
RSA (JWT RS/PS)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:6 high
RSA (JWT RS/PS)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:7 high
RSA (Web Crypto)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:56 high
RSA (Web Crypto)jsonwebtoken/lib/validateAsymmetricKey.js:60 high
ECDSA (JWT ES)jsonwebtoken/sign.js:14 high

Full inventory delivered as CycloneDX 1.6 CBOM + CSV.

Compliance Gap Analysis

Mapped against the frameworks in scope. Status reflects cryptographic-control readiness for post-quantum requirements.

FrameworkRelevant control areaStatusCoverageHeadline gap
FISMASC-13 cryptographic protection (OMB M-23-02 inventory) Fail 17%Cryptographic Protection: Replace quantum-vulnerable public-key crypto with NIST PQC standards (ML-KEM, ML-DSA).
CISAPQC inventory & prioritized migration plan Fail 33%Prioritized Migration Plan: Execute prioritized migration for critical/high assets within the agency PQC timeline.
FedRAMPSC-8/SC-13 transmission & use of cryptography Fail 33%Transmission Confidentiality & Integrity: Deploy hybrid TLS (classical + ML-KEM) and re-issue certs with PQC signatures.
SOC2CC6.1 logical access — encryption of data Fail 0%Logical Access — Encryption of Data: Replace quantum-vulnerable public-key algorithms guarding protected data with NIST PQC standards (ML-KEM, ML-DSA).
PCI-DSSReq. 3/4 — strong cryptography at rest & in transit Fail 0%Protect Stored Account Data with Strong Cryptography: Migrate stored-data protection to AES-256-GCM and NIST PQC public-key schemes (ML-KEM/ML-DSA); retire legacy ciphers.
CNSA-2.0Cryptographic controls Fail 20%Key Establishment — ML-KEM-1024: Replace RSA key transport and (EC)DH key exchange with ML-KEM-1024.
NIST-CSF-2.0Cryptographic controls Fail 20%Vulnerability Identification: Record quantum-vulnerable algorithms as crypto vulnerabilities and track them to remediation.

Auditor-ready evidence (JSON system-of-record + per-framework reports) accompanies this assessment.

Recommended Migration Roadmap

Stop the bleeding (0–3 months)Migrate the 168 critical/high-priority assets to hybrid PQC (ML-KEM for key exchange, ML-DSA for signing). Add QuantumVault to CI with --fail-on high so no new quantum-vulnerable crypto merges.
Systematic migration (3–12 months)Work the 127 medium-priority findings by service, re-issue certificates as hybrid X.509, and track progress to completion. Re-scan to measure drift.
Continuous assurance (ongoing)Stand up continuous monitoring and scheduled compliance evidence. Maintain the CBOM as a living system of record for auditors and regulators.

Methodology & caveats

Findings were produced by the open-source QuantumVault scanner (pattern-based detection across 25+ file types) over the scope above. Detection identifies uses of quantum-vulnerable cryptography, not exploitable vulnerabilities. Risk scores are a transparent, tunable 5-factor heuristic calibrated by deployment context — a prioritization aid, not a guarantee. Full raw output (CBOM, SARIF, CSV) accompanies this report.

Generated by QuantumVault
